# Ari Harrison

> Architect of the Intelligence Layer

## About

The gap between foundation models and real decisions is architecture. Not prompts. Not wrappers. Prediction layers over 122M+ indexed compounds, compliance systems that understand jurisdictional complexity, orchestration that gives models the specialized tools they need to move from conversation to computation.

NovoQuantNexus builds computational infrastructure for drug discovery and materials science. Two MCP servers — NovoMCP for discovery intelligence and Novo Compute for GPU simulation — expose 62 tools to any AI assistant. From target identification to patient stratification. From OLED screening to electrolyte stability. One platform, two verticals.

NovoWorkbench is the desktop surface. Researchers paste a molecule and get 3D structure, pharmacokinetic predictions, regulatory screening, and AI-optimized analogs — in under 2 seconds. Model-agnostic. Local-first. Signed and notarized by Apple.

Before this: autonomous discovery systems in high-stakes manufacturing — reinforcement learning agents that diagnosed failures and improved without intervention. The work is AI infrastructure. The domain is wherever the stakes are high enough to demand it.

## The Stack

- Intelligence Layer: 122M+ compounds, 84 properties each, sub-50ms
- Compliance: FAVES V4 — 8 jurisdictions, 1,585 structural alerts
- MCP Tools: 62 tools across 2 servers (NovoMCP + Novo Compute)
- Verticals: Drug discovery + materials science
- Desktop Surface: NovoWorkbench — local-first, model-agnostic
- Ecosystem: NVIDIA Inception, AWS Activate, Microsoft Founders Hub

## Publications

- NovoExpert: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction on TDC Benchmarks (2026, ChemRxiv) — Five SOTA wins on Therapeutics Data Commons ADMET endpoints: CYP2D6 (0.778 AUPRC), CYP3A4 (0.916 AUPRC), CYP3A4 Substrate (0.648 AUPRC), Clearance Hepatocyte (0.602 Spearman), DILI (0.922 AUROC). CatBoost ensembles with MapLight and GIN fingerprints. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15000061/v2

- Molecular Targets Shape Reasoning Topology: Bidirectional Validation of Chemistry-Inspired Bond Classification for AI Reasoning Chains (2026, ChemRxiv) — Across 478 reasoning traces from five ADMET endpoints, molecular targets determine AI reasoning topology with large effect sizes (Cohen's d = 0.94 and 1.75, both p < 10^-9). DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15001019/v1

## Projects

- NovoMCP + Novo Compute: 62 tools across two MCP servers. NovoMCP handles discovery intelligence — 122M+ compounds, ADMET predictions, FAVES V4 compliance, research search, lead optimization, funnel orchestration. Novo Compute handles simulation — GPU docking, GROMACS MD, quantum chemistry, neural network potentials, structure prediction, materials science. https://www.novoquantnexus.com/novomcp / https://www.novoquantnexus.com/novo-compute

- NovoWorkbench — The Desktop Surface: 3D rendering, pharmacokinetic predictions, regulatory screening, AI-optimized analogs in under 2 seconds. Model-agnostic. Local-first. Apple signed/notarized.

- FAVES V4 — Compliance Inline: Three-tier detection with 1,585 structural alert patterns, 8 jurisdictions, BOILED-Egg permeability, prior art checks. 100% sensitivity/specificity on 102-compound benchmark.

- Scaling MCP: Lessons from 122M Compounds (2026.02)
- From Conversational to Computational AI (2026.02)

Writing available at: https://substack.com/@ariharrison
